wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the YouTube API Dart SDK project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a native Dart interface to multiple YouTube REST APIs, its architecture as a multi-package monorepo, and relationship to YouTube's Data and Live Streaming APIs. Document the implementation approach using Retrofit for API endpoints, Dio for HTTP client, and cross-platform support. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the core functionality and package ecosystem. Document the five main packages (yt, yt_cli, yt_js, yt_mcp, yt_mcp_js) and their roles in the overall architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcast Lifecycle Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for broadcast lifecycle operations in YouTube Live Streaming API. Document the complete broadcast creation process including initial broadcast setup, configuration options, and metadata specification. Explain broadcast status transitions from created to testing, live, and completed states. Cover broadcast deletion and cleanup procedures. Include practical examples for creating broadcasts with different privacy settings (public, private, unlisted), configuring broadcast details like title, description, and scheduling, and managing broadcast lifecycle events. Address common lifecycle scenarios, error handling during state transitions, and best practices for broadcast management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Key Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for API key authentication in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain how to configure and use API keys for read-only operations such as video search, channel information retrieval, and public content access. Document the Yt.withApiKey() factory constructor, API key configuration options, and limitations of API key authentication. Include practical examples showing how to initialize the SDK with API keys, handle rate limits, and implement proper error handling. Address security considerations, API key storage best practices, and common troubleshooting scenarios for API key authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stream Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for YouTube Live Stream lifecycle management. Detail the complete stream creation process including initial stream setup, configuration parameters, and validation requirements. Explain stream update operations for modifying stream properties, ingestion settings, and technical specifications. Document stream deletion procedures and cleanup processes. Cover stream state transitions, validation rules, and error handling throughout the lifecycle. Include practical examples for creating streams with different configurations, updating stream metadata, and safely deleting unused streams. Address stream ownership management, content owner permissions, and channel association requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Live Chat Messages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for live chat message functionality. Document the LiveChatMessage model structure including snippet and authorDetails properties. Explain message listing operations with pagination support, filtering options, and real-time message retrieval. Cover message insertion methods, text message formatting, and emoji handling. Detail the message lifecycle from posting to deletion, including validation rules and error handling. Provide practical examples for retrieving chat history, implementing message filters, and handling different message types. Address message metadata, user permissions, and content moderation integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Yt Class Entry Point
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Yt class as the main entry point of the YouTube API Dart SDK. Thoroughly explain the factory constructors pattern used for authentication including Yt.withApiKey(), Yt.withOAuth(), and Yt.withTokenGenerator(). Document the initialization process, parameter requirements, and authentication flow setup. Include concrete examples showing different authentication scenarios and their use cases. Explain the relationship between the Yt class and individual API modules, how it manages shared configuration, and the role of interceptors in the authentication process. Address common initialization patterns, error handling during setup, and best practices for different deployment environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for video operations management. Document comprehensive video CRUD operations including upload, metadata management, privacy controls, and deletion. Explain resumable video uploads with progress tracking, multipart upload handling, and file validation. Cover video metadata operations including snippet updates, status modifications, content details management, and category assignments. Detail video rating systems, abuse reporting mechanisms, and bulk video operations. Include practical examples for video upload workflows, metadata batch updates, privacy setting changes, and video lifecycle management. Address video optimization techniques, thumbnail management, and content ownership considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dart MCP Server Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dart-based MCP server implementation. Explain the YtMcpServer class architecture, tool annotations, and environment-based authentication setup. Document all available YouTube API tools including channels_list, search_list, videos_list, playlists_list, comments_list, and comment_threads_list. Include practical examples of tool invocation, parameter configuration, and response handling. Address the bootstrapFromEnv method for credential management, static client persistence across tool calls, and error handling patterns. Provide guidance on building and running the MCP server, integrating with AI assistants, and extending functionality with custom tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Browser Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive web browser integration documentation for the yt JavaScript bindings. Explain how to integrate the compiled Dart package into web applications using standard HTML script tags or modern bundlers. Document the authentication flow for browser environments, including OAuth redirect handling and credential storage considerations. Provide practical examples for embedding YouTube functionality in web pages, handling CORS restrictions, and managing API keys securely in client-side applications. Include guidance on webpack configuration, CDN usage, and performance optimization for browser environments. Address common browser compatibility issues and debugging techniques for web-based YouTube API integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thumbnail Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive thumbnail management documentation for YouTube thumbnail operations. Detail the thumbnail upload process including resumable uploads, supported thumbnail sizes (default, medium, high, standard, maxres), and quality considerations. Document the set() method for uploading custom thumbnails to videos, including File parameter handling, upload URI generation, and error scenarios. Explain thumbnail URL retrieval, thumbnail selection strategies, and best practices for thumbnail optimization. Include practical examples of thumbnail upload workflows, error handling patterns, and integration with video management processes. Address thumbnail dimensions, aspect ratios, and platform-specific considerations for optimal display across different devices and screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Browser Support
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for web browser support in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain the OAuth implementation for browser environments using googleapis_auth and BrowserClient. Document the authentication flow differences in web browsers, including popup-based OAuth flows and token storage mechanisms. Cover browser-specific considerations such as CORS restrictions, HTTPS requirements, and browser compatibility. Include practical examples of setting up OAuth in web applications, handling browser-based authentication callbacks, and managing access tokens in client-side applications. Address security considerations for browser environments, token persistence strategies, and debugging techniques specific to web deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: OAuth Flow Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for OAuth 2.0 flow implementation in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain the complete authorization flow from client registration to user consent, including redirect URI configuration, scope specification, and authorization code exchange. Document the OAuthAccessControl factory constructor and how it selects platform-specific implementations. Detail the authorization URL construction, state parameter handling, and PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) implementation. Include step-by-step examples for setting up OAuth clients in Google Cloud Console, configuring redirect URIs, and implementing the authorization callback handler. Address common OAuth flow issues, security considerations, and best practices for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the YouTube API Dart SDK. Include installation instructions for all platforms (Dart/Flutter, CLI, JavaScript/TypeScript). Document the three main authentication methods: API key, OAuth 2.0, and token generator. Provide step-by-step setup examples for each package type. Include basic usage patterns for creating YouTube client instances, making simple API calls, and handling common scenarios. Document prerequisite knowledge including Dart programming fundamentals, RESTful API concepts, and OAuth 2.0 authentication principles. Provide quick start examples for immediate productivity and link to more detailed sections for deeper understanding.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcast Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for broadcast status management in YouTube Live Streaming API. Detail all broadcast states including created, testing, live, completed, and upcoming. Explain the transition methods (transition function) and their effects on broadcast behavior. Document status-specific configurations like monitor stream activation during testing, live stream initiation, and broadcast completion procedures. Include practical examples for transitioning between states, handling status validation errors, and implementing status monitoring workflows. Address common status-related issues, timing considerations for state changes, and best practices for managing broadcast availability and scheduling.

wiki_catalogs.name: OAuth 2.0 Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for OAuth 2.0 authentication implementation in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Detail the Yt.withOAuth() factory constructor, OAuth flow configuration, and token management strategies. Explain the OAuthAccessControlInterface and its platform-specific implementations for web and native Dart applications. Document the complete OAuth authorization flow, token refresh mechanisms, scope configuration, and consent handling. Include step-by-step examples for setting up OAuth clients, handling user authorization, managing access tokens, and implementing proper token storage and refresh logic. Address security considerations, token expiration handling, and common OAuth-related errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stream Health and Performance Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for live stream health monitoring and performance tracking. Explain how to monitor stream status, ingestion quality, and technical performance metrics. Document stream statistics collection, health indicators, and performance benchmarks. Detail alerting mechanisms for stream failures, quality degradation, and technical issues. Include examples of monitoring stream ingestion URLs, tracking stream latency, and identifying connectivity problems. Address stream quality optimization, bandwidth monitoring, and troubleshooting performance bottlenecks. Provide guidance on setting up automated monitoring systems and integrating with external monitoring tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Moderation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for chat moderation tools and capabilities. Detail message deletion operations, user timeout functionality, and ban management features. Explain moderation workflows including identifying problematic content, applying penalties, and maintaining community standards. Document the integration with Chatbot for automated moderation, including dialog-based responses and intelligent message filtering. Cover moderation best practices, community management strategies, and enforcement policies. Provide examples for implementing custom moderation rules, handling spam detection, and managing user interactions during live streams.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Modules Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the modular API structure within the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain how each API module (broadcast, live_stream, chat, videos, playlists, channels, etc.) is organized as a separate class extending the YouTubeApiHelper base class. Document the module responsibilities, public interfaces, and how they interact with the shared HTTP client. Include examples of module instantiation, method signatures, and response handling patterns. Explain the consistency patterns across modules, common error handling approaches, and how new modules can be added following the established structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Playlist Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for playlist management operations. Document complete playlist CRUD functionality including creation, modification, organization, and deletion. Explain playlist item management operations including adding, removing, reordering, and bulk playlist item manipulations. Cover playlist metadata operations such as title updates, description changes, privacy settings, and scheduling. Detail playlist item positioning strategies, automatic playlist generation, and playlist analytics. Include practical examples for playlist creation workflows, bulk item additions, playlist organization patterns, and playlist sharing configurations. Address playlist performance optimization, item deduplication, and playlist migration strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Node.js MCP Server Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Node.js-compatible MCP server implementation. Explain the differences between the Dart and Node.js versions, including platform-specific considerations and deployment scenarios. Document the JavaScript/TypeScript bindings, build processes, and integration patterns with Node.js environments. Include practical examples of setting up the MCP server in Node.js applications, handling environment variables, and connecting with AI assistants. Address compatibility considerations, performance optimizations, and migration paths from the Dart implementation. Provide guidance on extending functionality and maintaining consistency across both server implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Node.js Server Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed Node.js server integration documentation for the yt JavaScript bindings. Explain installation via npm/yarn, ES6 module imports, and CommonJS require statements. Document server-side authentication patterns, environment variable management, and credential security best practices. Provide practical examples for Express.js applications, serverless functions, and microservice architectures. Include guidance on handling rate limits, implementing retry logic, and managing long-running operations. Address memory management, error handling patterns, and performance optimization for Node.js environments. Cover integration with popular Node.js frameworks and deployment considerations for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subscription Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed subscription management documentation covering YouTube channel subscription operations. Document subscription creation, listing, and deletion workflows including proper authorization requirements and permission handling. Explain subscription resource structure, snippet properties, and contentDetails for managing subscription relationships. Detail pagination handling for subscription lists, filtering options, and subscription state management. Include practical examples of subscribing to channels, retrieving subscription feeds, managing multiple subscriptions, and handling subscription-related errors. Address subscription analytics, subscriber count management, and integration patterns with content discovery systems. Document best practices for subscription-based content curation and automated subscription workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Native Dart Applications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for native Dart application support in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain the OAuth implementation for Dart VM environments using googleapis_auth and IO HttpClient. Document the authentication flow for desktop and server applications, including credential storage, refresh token handling, and offline access scenarios. Cover platform-specific considerations such as file system access for credential storage, network connectivity requirements, and background processing capabilities. Include practical examples of setting up OAuth in native Dart applications, implementing service accounts for server-to-server authentication, and handling long-running processes. Address security best practices for credential storage, token refresh strategies, and production deployment considerations for native Dart applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for OAuth token management in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Detail access token storage strategies, refresh token handling, and automatic token renewal mechanisms. Explain the AccessCredentials object structure, token expiration detection, and secure storage options for different platforms. Document token refresh workflows, error handling for expired tokens, and fallback mechanisms. Include practical examples of implementing token persistence using platform-specific storage (SharedPreferences for mobile, localStorage for web), token validation patterns, and secure credential management. Address token lifecycle management, memory cleanup, and security best practices for token storage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain the three authentication methods: API key authentication for read-only operations, OAuth 2.0 for full access with user consent, and token generator for custom authentication flows. Document the factory pattern implementation in the Yt class with withApiKey(), withOAuth(), and withTokenGenerator() methods. Detail platform-specific OAuth implementations for web browsers and native Dart applications. Include authentication flow diagrams, token management strategies, refresh token handling, and security considerations. Provide practical examples for each authentication method with proper error handling and best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcast-Stream Binding Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for broadcast-stream binding operations in YouTube Live Streaming API. Document the bind/unbind functionality that connects broadcasts to video streams. Explain the relationship between broadcasts and streams, including one-to-one broadcast-stream relationships and multi-broadcast stream support. Cover stream binding procedures, validation requirements, and error handling for binding operations. Include practical examples for binding streams to broadcasts, unbinding existing bindings, and managing stream availability. Address common binding scenarios, troubleshooting binding issues, and best practices for stream preparation and broadcast coordination workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Token Generator Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for token generator authentication in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain the Yt.withTokenGenerator() factory constructor and custom authentication flow implementation. Document how to implement custom token generators for specialized authentication scenarios, JWT token handling, and integration with existing authentication systems. Include practical examples showing custom token generator implementation, token validation, and error handling strategies. Address use cases for enterprise authentication, custom OAuth providers, and integration with existing security infrastructure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ingestion URL Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for YouTube Live Stream ingestion URL management. Explain how to retrieve and configure ingestion URLs for stream input. Document ingestion URL types, formats, and security considerations. Detail the process of obtaining stream ingestion addresses, managing URL lifecycles, and handling URL rotation. Include examples of configuring different ingestion protocols, managing multiple ingestion endpoints, and implementing failover scenarios. Address ingestion URL security, access control, and best practices for URL distribution. Provide guidance on troubleshooting ingestion connectivity issues and optimizing ingestion performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Bots
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for chat bot integration and automation capabilities. Document the Chatbot class functionality including dialog management, question answering systems, and automated response generation. Explain the answerBot workflow for processing live chat messages and generating appropriate responses. Cover dialog configuration, response templates, and conversation flow management. Detail integration patterns with external AI services, natural language processing, and machine learning models. Provide practical examples for building custom chat bots, implementing FAQ systems, and automating community engagement. Address bot deployment strategies, performance optimization, and monitoring capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTTP Client Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the HTTP client configuration and request handling system. Document the Dio HTTP client setup, interceptor chain configuration, and authentication middleware implementation. Explain the logging interceptors, caching strategies, and error handling mechanisms. Include examples of custom interceptor creation, request/response transformation, and debugging techniques. Address timeout configurations, retry policies, and connection management. Document the relationship between HTTP client configuration and authentication flows, proxy support, and platform-specific networking considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Channel Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for channel management operations. Document channel information retrieval, profile customization, and channel settings management. Explain channel branding operations including banner updates, avatar changes, and channel customization options. Cover channel statistics tracking, subscriber management, and channel analytics integration. Detail channel monetization settings, community guidelines management, and channel visibility controls. Include practical examples for channel setup workflows, brand asset management, channel performance monitoring, and channel policy compliance. Address channel growth strategies, audience engagement tactics, and channel optimization best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: TypeScript Definitions and Type Safety
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed TypeScript definitions documentation for the yt JavaScript bindings. Explain the available type definitions, interface structures, and generic type parameters. Document type-safe usage patterns for all API methods, authentication flows, and callback handlers. Provide examples of proper type annotations, interface implementations, and type guards. Include guidance on extending existing types, creating custom type definitions, and handling optional properties. Address common TypeScript compilation issues, strict mode configurations, and integration with popular TypeScript projects. Document the relationship between Dart types and their JavaScript/TypeScript equivalents, including null safety considerations and runtime type checking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Categories
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive video category documentation covering YouTube video category lookup and management. Detail the category lookup process including regional variations, category ID mapping, and category hierarchy structures. Document category filtering options, category-based content organization, and category assignment workflows. Explain category metadata including category names, localized descriptions, and regional availability. Include practical examples of category-based content discovery, category filtering in search operations, and category management for content organization. Address category localization considerations, category ID persistence across regions, and integration patterns with content categorization systems. Document best practices for category selection, content classification, and automated category assignment strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Node.js Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Node.js integration support in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain the JavaScript/TypeScript bindings and MCP server implementation for Node.js environments. Document the authentication flow differences in Node.js, including server-side OAuth handling and credential management. Cover Node.js-specific considerations such as environment variable configuration, package installation and setup, and integration with existing Node.js applications. Include practical examples of using the SDK in Node.js applications, implementing chatbots with MCP protocol, and integrating with popular Node.js frameworks. Address performance optimization for server-side operations, memory management considerations, and production deployment strategies for Node.js applications using the YouTube API Dart SDK.

wiki_catalogs.name: Platform-Specific Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for platform-specific OAuth implementations in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Compare and contrast the OAuthAccessControlWeb and OAuthAccessControlIO implementations, highlighting differences in authentication mechanisms, storage capabilities, and user interaction patterns. Document browser-based OAuth flow for web applications, including popup handling, redirect-based flows, and iframe considerations. Explain native Dart implementation specifics for server-side applications, including credential file handling, service account authentication, and headless environment considerations. Include platform-specific configuration requirements, limitations, and recommended approaches for each target environment. Address cross-platform compatibility, conditional imports, and runtime platform detection mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Library Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the core YouTube API Dart SDK library. Describe the high-level design centered around the Yt class as the main entry point, the modular API structure with dedicated modules for different YouTube API endpoints, and the shared YouTubeApiHelper base class. Document component interactions between the Yt orchestrator, individual API modules, authentication interceptors, and HTTP client configuration. Explain technical decisions including the use of Retrofit for code generation, Dio for HTTP operations, and the strategy pattern for platform-specific implementations. Include system context diagrams showing the relationship between core components, data flow patterns, and integration points with external services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcast Querying and Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for broadcast querying and filtering capabilities in YouTube Live Streaming API. Detail the list method parameters including broadcastStatus filtering (active, upcoming, completed), broadcastType restrictions (all, owned, watch), and ID-based queries. Explain pagination support through pageToken and maxResults parameters. Document filtering by ownership (mine parameter) and content owner channels. Include practical examples for retrieving active broadcasts, upcoming broadcasts, specific broadcast IDs, and filtered results by various criteria. Address query optimization, performance considerations for large broadcast lists, and best practices for broadcast discovery and management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Platform-Specific Considerations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for platform-specific authentication considerations in the YouTube API Dart SDK. Compare and contrast OAuth implementation differences between web browsers and native Dart applications. Document browser-specific OAuth handling, redirect URI configuration, local storage management, and security considerations for web environments. Explain native application OAuth implementation, credential storage, and platform-specific token persistence. Include practical examples for each platform, addressing common issues such as CORS handling, browser compatibility, and native app security requirements. Provide troubleshooting guides for platform-specific authentication problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stream-Broadcast Binding
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for binding live streams to broadcasts. Explain the relationship between streams and broadcasts, including one-to-many binding scenarios. Document the stream binding process, including binding/unbinding operations and state management. Detail broadcast association requirements, binding validation, and conflict resolution. Include practical examples for binding streams to active broadcasts, managing multiple stream bindings, and handling binding errors. Address stream availability checks, broadcast readiness verification, and troubleshooting binding issues. Provide guidance on stream lifecycle coordination with broadcast schedules and automated binding workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Analytics & History
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for chat analytics and historical data management. Document the downloadHistory functionality including CSV export capabilities, timestamp-based filtering, and incremental data retrieval. Explain the TimeStore mechanism for tracking last processed messages and enabling efficient batch processing. Cover chat history analysis techniques, sentiment analysis integration, and engagement metrics calculation. Detail file-based storage options, memory management for large datasets, and data persistence strategies. Provide examples for implementing chat analytics dashboards, trend analysis, and community behavior insights. Address data privacy considerations, retention policies, and compliance requirements for chat data handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: YouTubeApiHelper Base Class
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the YouTubeApiHelper base class that provides common functionality across all API modules. Explain the template method pattern implementation, shared HTTP operations, and common response processing logic. Document the helper methods for request building, parameter validation, response mapping, and error standardization. Include examples of how individual modules extend the base class and override specific behaviors. Address the provider pattern used for API endpoint definitions, code generation integration, and how the base class facilitates consistent API interaction patterns throughout the SDK.

wiki_catalogs.name: Comment Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for comment management operations. Document comprehensive comment moderation workflows including comment approval, deletion, and user management. Explain comment thread operations for hierarchical comment conversations, reply management, and conversation threading. Cover comment analytics, engagement metrics, and comment spam detection. Detail comment filtering strategies, automated moderation tools, and manual review processes. Include practical examples for comment moderation workflows, bulk comment operations, comment thread management, and community guideline enforcement. Address comment system optimization, user reputation management, and comment-based content discovery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dart to JavaScript Compilation Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Dart to JavaScript compilation process used in the yt_js package. Explain the dart2js compilation pipeline, optimization flags, and build configuration options. Document the relationship between the Dart source code and generated JavaScript output, including code splitting and tree shaking considerations. Provide guidance on build optimization, bundle size reduction, and development vs production builds. Include troubleshooting common compilation issues, debugging compiled JavaScript, and maintaining compatibility across different JavaScript environments. Address integration with various build tools, CI/CD pipelines, and automated deployment processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Watermark Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed watermark management documentation for YouTube branded watermark configuration and positioning. Document watermark creation, upload, and positioning workflows including coordinate systems, scaling options, and timing controls. Explain watermark resource structure, target area configuration, and temporal positioning for watermark display. Detail watermark customization options including opacity settings, sizing algorithms, and responsive positioning. Include practical examples of watermark upload processes, positioning calculations, timing configurations, and multi-position watermark setups. Address watermark optimization for different video resolutions, aspect ratios, and playback contexts. Document best practices for watermark branding, legal considerations, and integration with content protection workflows. Explain watermark removal processes and management of watermark policies across different content types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcast Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive broadcast management documentation for YouTube Live Streaming API. Detail the complete broadcast lifecycle including creation, configuration, status management, and termination. Document the LiveBroadcastItem model and its properties for broadcast metadata. Explain broadcast states (created, testing, live, completed), broadcast configurations (status, content protection, recording settings), and broadcast scheduling. Include practical examples for creating broadcasts with different privacy settings, updating broadcast details, binding streams to broadcasts, and handling broadcast status transitions. Address common broadcast scenarios, error handling strategies, and best practices for live streaming workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Layer and Serialization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the data model layer and automatic serialization system. Document the generated model classes for API responses and requests, explaining the json_annotation usage and serialization patterns. Include examples of model instantiation, field mapping, and data transformation processes. Explain the relationship between raw API responses and typed Dart models, error handling for malformed data, and version compatibility considerations. Address the utility functions for data processing, enum handling, and common model manipulation patterns used throughout the SDK.

wiki_catalogs.name: Live Streaming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed live streaming documentation for YouTube Live Streaming API integration. Explain stream creation, configuration, and management throughout the live streaming lifecycle. Document the LiveStreamItem model covering stream properties, ingestion settings, and technical specifications. Detail stream health monitoring, ingestion URL management, and troubleshooting common streaming issues. Include practical examples for configuring stream inputs, managing multiple stream sources, handling stream interruptions, and optimizing stream quality. Address stream binding to broadcasts, stream statistics monitoring, and integration with broadcast management workflows. Provide guidance on stream testing, quality assurance, and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive chat management documentation for YouTube Live Chat integration. Detail real-time chat functionality including message sending, moderation, and user interaction patterns. Document the LiveChatMessage model and chat moderation features including message removal, user timeouts, and ban management. Explain WebSocket-based real-time chat connections, event handling, and chat bot integration capabilities. Include practical examples for monitoring chat activity, implementing automated moderation, creating chat bots, and managing chat settings. Address chat analytics, message filtering, user permission management, and integration with live streaming workflows. Provide guidance on chat moderation best practices and community management strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content management documentation for YouTube Data API operations. Cover the four main content categories: videos, playlists, channels, and comments. Document CRUD operations for each content type including creation, modification, deletion, and retrieval. Explain content metadata management, privacy controls, categorization, and organization strategies. Include practical examples for video uploads, playlist management, channel customization, and comment moderation. Address content discovery features, search integration, and content lifecycle management. Provide guidance on content organization best practices, bulk operations, and content migration strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search & Discovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed search and discovery documentation for YouTube Data API search functionality. Explain search query construction, filtering options, and result pagination. Document search parameters including content types (videos, channels, playlists), geographic targeting, date ranges, and quality filters. Include practical examples for building advanced search queries, implementing search result ranking, and handling search result pagination. Address search analytics, trending content discovery, and personalized search recommendations. Provide guidance on search optimization, query best practices, and integration with content recommendation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Modules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive utility module documentation for supplementary YouTube API features. Document each utility module's specific functionality, configuration options, and integration patterns. Include practical examples for thumbnail operations, subscription workflows, category management, and watermark positioning. Address utility module best practices, performance considerations, and common use cases in content management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: CLI Tool
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CLI tool documentation for the yt_cli package. Document all available commands, options, and configuration parameters for automating YouTube API operations. Explain command syntax, parameter validation, and output formatting. Include practical examples for common automation scenarios including batch operations, scheduled tasks, and integration with CI/CD pipelines. Address CLI configuration, credential management, and environment setup. Provide guidance on scripting patterns, error handling in automated contexts, and integration with other development tools. Document CLI best practices for production environments and troubleshooting common automation issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: JavaScript/TypeScript Bindings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed JavaScript/TypeScript bindings documentation for web and Node.js integration. Explain the Dart to JavaScript compilation process and available TypeScript definitions. Document usage patterns for browser applications, Node.js server-side integration, and hybrid application development. Include practical examples for web integration, authentication flows in browser environments, and server-side YouTube API operations. Address cross-platform considerations, dependency management, and performance optimization for JavaScript environments. Provide guidance on integrating with existing JavaScript frameworks, handling asynchronous operations, and managing credentials securely in client-side applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: MCP Server Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration documentation for AI assistant and chatbot applications. Explain the MCP protocol implementation, tool definitions for YouTube API operations, and integration patterns with AI assistants. Document the yt_mcp and yt_mcp_js packages for Dart and Node.js environments respectively. Include practical examples for creating YouTube-related tools, configuring MCP servers, and integrating with popular AI platforms. Address tool definition schemas, capability negotiation, and secure credential management for AI-assisted YouTube operations. Provide guidance on extending MCP functionality, custom tool development, and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cross-Platform Support
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive cross-platform support documentation for the YouTube API Dart SDK. Explain platform-specific implementations for web browsers, native Dart applications, and Node.js environments. Document OAuth implementation differences across platforms, authentication flow variations, and platform-specific considerations. Include practical examples for each platform showing authentication setup, API usage patterns, and deployment considerations. Address platform limitations, performance characteristics, and optimization strategies. Provide guidance on choosing the right platform for specific use cases, handling platform differences in code, and maintaining consistent functionality across all supported environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development & Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development and testing documentation for the YouTube API Dart SDK monorepo. Document the Melos workspace setup, dependency management across multiple packages, and development workflow. Explain code generation processes using build_runner, analysis configuration with strict linting rules, and testing strategies across all packages. Include practical examples for local development setup, running tests, code formatting, and continuous integration. Address contribution guidelines, code review processes, and release procedures. Provide guidance on debugging techniques, performance profiling, and maintaining code quality standards across the multi-package ecosystem.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# YouTube API Dart SDK - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The YouTube API Dart SDK is a comprehensive native Dart interface designed to provide seamless access to multiple YouTube REST APIs, including the YouTube Data API and Live Streaming API. This project serves as a unified solution for developers building applications that need to interact with YouTube's extensive video platform capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Unified API Access**: Provide a cohesive interface for accessing YouTube's diverse API endpoints
- **Multi-Platform Support**: Enable usage across native Dart applications, CLI tools, web browsers, and Node.js environments
- **Authentication Flexibility**: Support both API key and OAuth 2.0 authentication methods
- **Comprehensive Coverage**: Implement all major YouTube API features including live streaming, chat moderation, and content management

### Target Audience
- Dart/Flutter developers building YouTube-integrated applications
- Backend developers requiring programmatic YouTube API access
- CLI tool developers needing automated YouTube operations
- AI/Chatbot developers utilizing YouTube Live Streaming capabilities

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a modular monorepo architecture with five distinct packages, each serving specific use cases:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[YT Workspace] --> B[YT Core Package]
    A --> C[YT CLI Package]
    A --> D[YT JS Package]
    A --> E[YT MCP Package]
    A --> F[YT MCP JS Package]
    
    B --> G[Broadcast Module]
    B --> H[Channels Module]
    B --> I[Chat Module]
    B --> J[Comments Module]
    B --> K[Live Stream Module]
    B --> L[Playlists Module]
    B --> M[Videos Module]
    
    N[Authentication Layer] --> O[OAuth Handler]
    N --> P[API Key Handler]
    N --> Q[Token Generator]
    
    R[Model Layer] --> S[Response Models]
    R --> T[Request Models]
    R --> U[Utility Classes]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Factory Pattern**: Used extensively in the `Yt` class for creating authenticated instances
- **Module Pattern**: Each API endpoint is encapsulated in dedicated modules (Broadcast, Channels, Chat, etc.)
- **Builder Pattern**: Authentication setup through fluent interfaces (`withApiKey`, `withOAuth`)
- **Singleton Pattern**: Centralized HTTP client management through Dio instance

### System Relationships
The architecture implements a layered approach with clear separation between authentication, business logic, and data models:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Application] --> B[Authentication Layer]
    B --> C[HTTP Client (Dio)]
    C --> D[YouTube API Endpoints]
    D --> E[Response Processing]
    E --> F[Model Objects]
    
    G[CLI Interface] --> B
    H[Web Interface] --> B
    I[MCP Server] --> B
    
    J[OAuth Flow] --> B
    K[API Key Flow] --> B
    L[Token Generation] --> B
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Core Library**: `packages/yt/lib/yt.dart` - Primary library exports and module organization
- **CLI Tool**: `packages/yt_cli/bin/yt.dart` - Command-line interface executable
- **MCP Server**: `packages/yt_mcp/bin/yt_mcp.dart` - AI/Chatbot MCP server implementation

### Core Modules
The project implements comprehensive coverage of YouTube's API ecosystem:

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Yt {
        +Dio dio
        +Broadcast broadcast
        +Channels channels
        +Chat chat
        +Comments comments
        +CommentThreads commentThreads
        +LiveStream liveStream
        +Playlists playlists
        +PlaylistItems playlistItems
        +Search search
        +Subscriptions subscriptions
        +Thumbnails thumbnails
        +Videos videos
        +VideoCategories videoCategories
        +Watermarks watermarks
        +withApiKey()
        +withOAuth()
        +withGenerator()
    }
    
    class Broadcast {
        +create()
        +update()
        +bind()
        +unbind()
    }
    
    class Chat {
        +getLiveChatMessages()
        +moderateChat()
        +sendMessage()
    }
    
    class LiveStream {
        +create()
        +update()
        +healthStatus()
    }
    
    Yt --> Broadcast
    Yt --> Chat
    Yt --> LiveStream
```

### Configuration Approach
- **Workspace Configuration**: Centralized in `pubspec.yaml` defining package relationships
- **Build Configuration**: `build.yaml` files in individual packages for code generation
- **Analysis Configuration**: `analysis_options.yaml` enforcing strict typing and linting rules

### External Dependencies
The project leverages a carefully selected set of dependencies:

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[YT Package] --> B[dio ^5.9.0]
    A --> C[googleapis_auth ^2.0.0]
    A --> D[retrofit ^4.7.2]
    A --> E[json_annotation ^4.9.0]
    
    F[YT CLI] --> A
    F --> G[args ^2.7.0]
    
    H[YT JS] --> A
    H --> I[web ^1.1.0]
    
    J[YT MCP] --> A
    J --> K[dart_mcp ^0.5.1]
    J --> L[easy_api_annotations ^0.6.0]
    
    M[YT MCP JS] --> J
    M --> N[stream_channel ^2.1.4]
    M --> O[web_socket_channel ^3.0.2]
```

### Integration Points
- **OAuth Integration**: Google APIs authentication flow
- **WebSocket Support**: Real-time chat and live streaming capabilities
- **Cross-Platform Compatibility**: Universal IO for native and web environments
- **Code Generation**: Retrofit and JSON serialization for type-safe API interactions

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The SDK provides comprehensive YouTube API functionality across multiple domains:

#### Live Streaming Capabilities
- **Broadcast Management**: Create, update, bind, and manage live broadcasts
- **Stream Health Monitoring**: Track and analyze live stream performance
- **Real-time Chat Integration**: Moderate and manage live chat interactions
- **Watermark Management**: Configure custom watermarks for branded streams

#### Content Management
- **Video Operations**: Upload, update, delete, and manage video content
- **Channel Management**: Access and modify channel information and settings
- **Playlist Operations**: Create, update, and organize video playlists
- **Comment Moderation**: Manage comments and comment threads

#### Search and Discovery
- **Advanced Search**: Comprehensive video, channel, and playlist search capabilities
- **Category Management**: Work with YouTube video categories and classifications
- **Subscription Management**: Handle user subscriptions and follow relationships

### Implementation Highlights
- **Authentication Abstraction**: Unified authentication interface supporting multiple auth methods
- **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error handling with specific exception types
- **Logging Integration**: Built-in logging support with configurable verbosity levels
- **Extensibility**: Modular design allowing for easy extension and customization

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> APIKey: withApiKey()
    Unauthenticated --> OAuth: withOAuth()
    Unauthenticated --> TokenGen: withGenerator()
    
    APIKey --> Ready: Authentication Success
    OAuth --> Ready: Token Acquired
    TokenGen --> Ready: Token Generated
    
    Ready --> BroadcastOps: Broadcast Module
    Ready --> ChatOps: Chat Module
    Ready --> StreamOps: Live Stream Module
    Ready --> ContentOps: Content Management
    
    BroadcastOps --> Ready
    ChatOps --> Ready
    StreamOps --> Ready
    ContentOps --> Ready
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
The YouTube API Dart SDK demonstrates excellent architectural design with its modular monorepo structure, comprehensive API coverage, and multi-platform support. The project successfully abstracts complex YouTube API interactions into a developer-friendly interface while maintaining flexibility and extensibility.

### Key Characteristics
- **Comprehensive Coverage**: Implements virtually all major YouTube API endpoints
- **Multi-Platform Support**: Native Dart, CLI, web, and Node.js compatibility
- **Robust Authentication**: Flexible authentication system supporting multiple OAuth flows
- **Type Safety**: Extensive use of code generation for compile-time safety
- **Performance Focus**: Efficient HTTP client integration with interceptors and caching

### Architectural Strengths
- Clear separation of concerns across modules
- Consistent error handling and logging patterns
- Extensible authentication system
- Comprehensive model layer with automatic serialization
- Well-structured monorepo with proper dependency management

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Documentation**: Enhanced inline documentation and examples
- **Testing Coverage**: Expanded unit and integration test suites
- **Performance Optimization**: Consider adding built-in caching mechanisms
- **Async Operations**: Improved async/await patterns throughout
- **Configuration Management**: Centralized configuration for different environments

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Enhance Documentation**: Add comprehensive inline documentation and usage examples
2. **Expand Testing**: Implement thorough testing strategies for all modules
3. **Performance Monitoring**: Add built-in metrics and performance monitoring
4. **Configuration Templates**: Provide example configurations for common use cases
5. **Migration Guides**: Document breaking changes and migration paths for future updates

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **AI Integration**: Leverage the MCP architecture for advanced AI-powered YouTube operations
- **Real-time Features**: Expand WebSocket support for real-time notifications and events
- **Plugin System**: Develop plugin architecture for extending functionality
- **Performance Analytics**: Implement comprehensive performance monitoring and optimization
- **Community Features**: Add support for community-driven content moderation tools

**Sources:**
- [pubspec.yaml](pubspec.yaml)
- [yt.dart](packages/yt/lib/yt.dart)
- [yt_base.dart](packages/yt/lib/src/yt_base.dart)
- [analysis_options.yaml](analysis_options.yaml)
- [packages/yt/pubspec.yaml](packages/yt/pubspec.yaml)
- [packages/yt_cli/pubspec.yaml](packages/yt_cli/pubspec.yaml)
- [packages/yt_js/pubspec.yaml](packages/yt_js/pubspec.yaml)
- [packages/yt_mcp/pubspec.yaml](packages/yt_mcp/pubspec.yaml)
- [packages/yt_mcp_js/pubspec.yaml](packages/yt_mcp_js/pubspec.yaml)

</blog>
